leaves.title: Evals: package scaffold with config and result models
leaves.content:
**What/why.** Create the `agents/evals/` package skeleton: settings and the typed result models every other eval leaf builds on. This is the eval harness that scores local models against frozen gold sets (see `architect-brief.md` and `pipeline-runs/distill-evals/framing.md`).

**Files.** `agents/evals/__init__.py` (empty), `agents/evals/config.py`, `agents/evals/models.py`, `agents/evals/tests/__init__.py`, `agents/evals/tests/test_models.py`.

**config.py** — `EvalsSettings(BaseSettings)` with `env_prefix="EVALS_"`, mirroring `agents/coding_pipeline/config.py` style: `goldsets_dir: Path` (default `<repo>/agents/evals/goldsets`), `runs_dir: Path` (default `<repo>/eval-runs`), `openai_base_url: str = "http://llm-router.internal:4010/v1"`, `openai_api_key: str = "sk-REDACTED"`, `model: str = "coder"`, `repeats: int = 3`, `temperature: float = 0.0`, `timeout: float = 240.0`, `max_tokens: int = 16_000`. Module-level `settings` instance.

**models.py** — Pydantic models:
- `GoldCase`: `step: Literal["replan" | "decompose" | "boundedness" | "review-findings" | "review-confirm" | "testgap-find" | "testgap-skeptic"]`, `case_id: str` (lowercase slug, validated), `case_dir: Path`, `schema_version: int`, `holdout: bool = False`, `inputs: dict[str, str]` (logical name -> filename inside case_dir), `expected: dict`, `notes: str = ""`.
- `GradeCheck`: `name: str`, `passed: bool`, `detail: str = ""`.
- `GradeResult`: `case_id`, `step`, `passed: bool`, `score: float` (0..1), `checks: list[GradeCheck]`, `error: str | None = None` (transport/parse-level failure).
- `CaseScore`: `case_id`, `holdout: bool`, `repeats: list[GradeResult]`, plus computed properties `passed_majority: bool` (strict majority of non-error repeats) and `mean_score: float`.
- `StepScore`: `step`, `cases: list[CaseScore]`, properties `pass_rate`, `holdout_pass_rate` (None-safe when no holdout cases), `error_repeats: int`.
- `Scorecard`: `model: str`, `timestamp: str`, `steps: list[StepScore]`, `notes: str = ""`, property `overall_pass_rate`.

**Acceptance criteria.**
- All models construct, validate, and JSON round-trip; `case_id` rejects non-slug values.
- `CaseScore.passed_majority` and the rate properties are unit-tested including the zero-cases / all-error edge cases (no ZeroDivisionError).
- `uv run pytest agents/evals` green; ruff clean (100-char lines).

boundedness.notes: in-session architect judgment (Fable) fills the boundedness seat

leaves.title: Evals: gold case loader with schema version pinning
leaves.content:
**What/why.** The fixture loader: gold cases are directories checked into the repo; the loader turns them into validated `GoldCase` objects and fails loudly on any drift — a gold set that silently stops loading is a dead regression harness.

**Files.** `agents/evals/fixtures.py`, `agents/evals/tests/test_fixtures.py`.

**Layout contract.** `goldsets/<step>/<case-id>/case.yaml` plus input files in the same directory. `case.yaml` fields: `schema_version: int`, `step: str` (must equal its parent dir name), `holdout: bool` (default false), `inputs: {logical_name: filename}`, `expected: {...}` (step-specific, opaque here), `notes: str`.

**fixtures.py.**
- `SUPPORTED_SCHEMA_VERSION = 1`.
- `class EvalFixtureError(RuntimeError)` — every raise names the offending case dir.
- `load_goldsets(root: Path, step: str | None = None) -> list[GoldCase]`: walk `root/<step>/<case-id>/`, parse `case.yaml`, validate: known step key, step field matches dir, `schema_version == SUPPORTED_SCHEMA_VERSION`, every `inputs` filename exists in the case dir. Returns cases sorted by (step, case_id) for deterministic runs.
- `read_input(case: GoldCase, name: str) -> str` — resolve + read a logical input.

**Acceptance criteria.**
- tmp_path-built fixtures load correctly; ordering is deterministic.
- Distinct tests for: unknown step dir, missing case.yaml, step/dir mismatch, wrong schema_version, missing input file — each raises EvalFixtureError naming the case.
- `uv run pytest agents/evals` green; ruff clean.
depends_on: Evals: package scaffold with config and result models

boundedness.notes: in-session architect judgment (Fable) fills the boundedness seat

leaves.title: Evals: step adapters rendering production prompts
leaves.content:
**What/why.** The harness must measure PRODUCTION reality: every scored call renders the exact system/user messages the agents send, built by the agents' own prompt code — never a copy that can drift. One adapter per graded prompt surface, keyed by step.

**Files.** `agents/evals/steps.py`, `agents/evals/tests/test_steps.py` (small wire models may go in `agents/evals/models.py` if cleaner).

**Interface.** `@dataclass PromptSpec: system: str; user: str; schema: type[BaseModel]`. `@dataclass StepAdapter: step: str; build: Callable[[GoldCase], PromptSpec]`. Module-level `ADAPTERS: dict[str, StepAdapter]` with exactly these keys:
- `replan` — inputs `framing.json`, `tree.json`, `report.json`, `attempts.json`. Parse into `FramingProposal` / `TaskTree` / `WaveReport` / `dict[str, int]`; compute `escalated` via `agents.coding_pipeline.architect.deterministic_escalations`; system = `REPLAN_SYSTEM`; user via `architect._replan_user(...)`; schema = `architect.ReplanEnvelope`.
- `decompose` — inputs `framing.json`, `inventory.json`; system = `DECOMPOSE_SYSTEM`; user via `architect._decompose_user`; schema = `TaskTree`.
- `boundedness` — input `leaf.json` (one LeafSpec); system = `BOUNDEDNESS_SYSTEM`; user via `architect._leaf_summary`; schema = `architect.LeafBoundedness`.
- `review-findings` — input `diff.patch`; system = `coding_pipeline.verify.FINDINGS_SYSTEM`; user = the same `f"Wave diff:\n\n{diff}"` shape `collect_findings` sends; schema = `verify.FindingsEnvelope`.
- `review-confirm` — inputs `diff.patch`, `candidate.json` (summary/file/severity); system = `verify.CONFIRM_SYSTEM`; user mirrors the `make_user` lambda in `verify.confirm_findings`; schema: `ConfirmVerdict(BaseModel)` = `{real: bool, reason: str}` (new wire model matching the prompt's declared JSON shape).
- `testgap-find` — inputs `context.md` (source + existing tests), `angle` key in `case.yaml` expected/inputs; system via `testing_ensemble.prompts.finder_system` with the matching entry from `FINDER_ANGLES`; user = context; schema: `GapsEnvelope` matching `_GAP_SHAPE` (`gaps: list[{target, gap_type, why_it_matters, suggested_test, severity}]`).
- `testgap-skeptic` — inputs `context.md`, `gap.json`; system = `prompts.SKEPTIC_BASE`; user via `prompts.verify_user`; schema: `SkepticVerdict` = `{real: bool, confidence: str, severity: str, reasoning: str}`.

Importing private helpers (`_replan_user`, `_decompose_user`, `_leaf_summary`) is the POINT — same repo, and it guarantees the eval renders what production renders. Do not duplicate their logic. Agent modules must not import `agents.evals` (dependency points evals -> agents only).

**Acceptance criteria.**
- For fixed inputs, each adapter's user string is byte-equal to the production builder's output (tests call both) and `system` is identity-equal (`is`) to the production constant.
- Unknown step key raises KeyError/ValueError with the step name.
- New wire models validate the exact JSON shapes the prompts declare.
- `uv run pytest agents/evals` green; ruff clean.
depends_on: Evals: package scaffold with config and result models

boundedness.notes: in-session architect judgment (Fable) fills the boundedness seat

leaves.title: Evals: replan action grader
leaves.content:
**What/why.** Deterministic grader for the `replan` step. Judges the model's RAW output — validity is part of the grade (the 3a6360cb incident: every coder replan was discarded as schema-invalid), so the grader never benefits from retry laundering.

**Files.** `agents/evals/graders/__init__.py`, `agents/evals/graders/replan.py`, `agents/evals/tests/test_grader_replan.py`.

**Contract.** `grade(case: GoldCase, raw: str) -> GradeResult`. Parse via `agents.shared.llm.extract_json` + `ReplanEnvelope.model_validate` (mirror `panel._parse_model` semantics). Unparseable/invalid -> `passed=False`, `score=0.0`, check `valid-envelope` failed, remaining checks skipped.

**Expected block** (`case.yaml` `expected:`): `must: [{kind, finding_slug?, leaf_title?, feature?}]` (an action matches on kind + every given target field), `forbid_kinds: [str]`, `forbid_targets: [str]` (leaf titles the model must not touch — the escalated list), `allow_extra: bool = false`, `require_empty: bool = false`.

**Checks** (each a named GradeCheck):
1. `valid-envelope` — parses + schema-validates.
2. `must-actions` — every `must` entry matched by exactly >=1 action.
3. `no-forbidden` — no action has a forbidden kind or targets (leaf_title / revised.title / leaf.title) a forbidden target.
4. `fixup-confirmed-only` — every fixup's `finding_slug` is a CONFIRMED finding slug in the case's `report.json` (read via the case inputs).
5. `no-extras` — when `allow_extra` false: no unmatched actions (skip-pass when true). When `require_empty`: actions list must be empty.
6. `leaf-floors` — every new/revised LeafSpec: estimate in {xs,s,m}; non-Manual leaves have `requires_tests=true`; content is non-trivial (>= 200 chars).

`score` = passed checks / total applicable; `passed` = all applicable checks pass.

**Acceptance criteria.**
- Unit tests cover: valid fixup-only answer passes; invalid JSON fails check 1 only; touching a forbidden target fails 3; fixup for unconfirmed slug fails 4; extra action fails 5 unless allow_extra; require_empty with clean wave; leaf floor violations fail 6.
- `uv run pytest agents/evals` green; ruff clean.
depends_on: Evals: package scaffold with config and result models

boundedness.notes: in-session architect judgment (Fable) fills the boundedness seat

leaves.title: Evals: decomposition and boundedness grader
leaves.content:
**What/why.** Grader for the `decompose` and `boundedness` steps. Decompositions can differ legitimately, so grading is structure + a frozen per-case rubric checklist (authored by Fable, applied deterministically) — never exact-tree match, never a live judge. Umbrella-leaf detection is the heart: the #1 local-tier failure.

**Files.** `agents/evals/graders/decomposition.py`, `agents/evals/tests/test_grader_decomposition.py`.

**Contract.** `grade_decompose(case, raw) -> GradeResult` and `grade_boundedness(case, raw) -> GradeResult` (dispatched by step key).

**Decompose structural checks** (always applied):
1. `valid-tree` — extract_json + `TaskTree.model_validate`, >=1 leaf.
2. `deps-resolve` — reuse `architect._validate_deps` (unknown titles / cycles fail).
3. `files-named` — every leaf's content contains >=1 path-like token (regex `[A-Za-z0-9_./-]+\.[a-z0-9]{1,5}` or a `Files` section) unless the leaf is `complexity="novel"` + Manual.
4. `estimates-bounded` — every leaf estimate in {xs,s,m} OR novel+Manual.
5. `auto-floors` — every non-Manual leaf: `requires_tests=true` and (`max_files` null or >= 3).

**Rubric DSL** (from `expected.rubric: [...]`; each item one GradeCheck named by its `id`):
`{id, kind, ...}` with kinds: `require_leaf {title_pattern?, content_pattern?}` (>=1 leaf matches all given regexes); `forbid_leaf {title_pattern?, content_pattern?}`; `max_leaves {n}` / `min_leaves {n}`; `require_dep {from_pattern, to_pattern}` (some matching edge exists); `require_manual {title_pattern}` (all matching leaves Manual). Unknown kind -> EvalFixtureError (fail loudly, not silently green).

**Boundedness check:** parse `LeafBoundedness`; compare `worker_shaped` to `expected.worker_shaped` (bool); when `expected.criteria` present, also compare each named criterion field. Score = fraction correct; passed = overall verdict correct.

**Acceptance criteria.**
- Tests: valid tree passing all structural checks; cycle fails 2; pathless content fails 3; xl estimate fails 4; Auto leaf with max_files=1 fails 5; each rubric kind has a pass and a fail test; unknown rubric kind raises; boundedness right/wrong verdicts scored.
- `uv run pytest agents/evals` green; ruff clean.
depends_on: Evals: package scaffold with config and result models

boundedness.notes: in-session architect judgment (Fable) fills the boundedness seat

leaves.title: Evals: review findings grader
leaves.content:
**What/why.** Grader for `review-findings` (precision/recall vs a frozen reference finding set over a known-buggy diff) and `review-confirm` (skeptic-vote accuracy on labeled real/decoy candidates). This is the generate-and-compare flagship: real bugs caught, no false alarms.

**Files.** `agents/evals/graders/review.py`, `agents/evals/tests/test_grader_review.py`.

**review-findings.** Parse `FindingsEnvelope` from raw (invalid -> score 0). `expected.must_find: [{id, file_pattern?, keywords_any: [str]?, keywords_all: [str]?}]` — a candidate finding matches a reference iff its `file` matches `file_pattern` (regex, skipped when absent; candidate file may be null only if pattern absent) AND case-insensitive keyword rules hold against `summary`. One candidate may satisfy only one reference (greedy best match). Metrics: `recall` = matched refs / refs; `precision` = matched candidates / candidates (empty candidate list with empty must_find = precision 1); `score` = F1. Thresholds from expected: `recall_min` (default 0.7), `precision_min` (default 0.5); `passed` = both met. Also check `max-findings` (prompt cap: <= 5) and severity values legal.

**review-confirm.** Parse `ConfirmVerdict`; compare `real` to `expected.real` (bool). Score 1/0; `passed` = correct. The decoy cases (expected.real=false: style nits, pre-existing issues) are where the local tier cries wolf — that is the judgment frozen here.

**Acceptance criteria.**
- Tests: perfect match; paraphrase matched via keywords; miss drops recall below floor; hallucinated finding drops precision; greedy matching never double-counts; empty/empty edge; confirm right + wrong on both labels.
- `uv run pytest agents/evals` green; ruff clean.
depends_on: Evals: package scaffold with config and result models

boundedness.notes: in-session architect judgment (Fable) fills the boundedness seat

leaves.title: Evals: test gap severity grader
leaves.content:
**What/why.** Grader for `testgap-find` (is the gap list sane — finds the real holes, does not cry wolf on trivia) and `testgap-skeptic` (labeled real/already-covered verdicts). Severity ORDERING matters as much as detection: a ranker that calls trivia critical drowns the real hole.

**Files.** `agents/evals/graders/testgap.py`, `agents/evals/tests/test_grader_testgap.py`.

**testgap-find.** Parse `GapsEnvelope` (invalid -> 0). Reference lists in expected: `must_find` (match rules identical in shape to the review grader: id + target_pattern / keywords) and `nice_to_find` (matches count for neither recall nor cry-wolf). Checks:
1. `recall` — matched must_find / must_find >= `recall_min` (default 0.7).
2. `cry-wolf` — candidates with severity high|critical matching NOTHING (neither list) must number <= `cry_wolf_max` (default 1).
3. `severity-order` — over matched must_find pairs, pairwise agreement between model severity ranks (critical>high>medium>low) and the reference ranks: concordant / (concordant + discordant), ties skipped, >= `order_min` (default 0.6; check skip-passes with < 2 comparable pairs).
Score = mean of the three check scores (each as its ratio, capped at 1).

**testgap-skeptic.** Parse `SkepticVerdict`; compare `real` to `expected.real`. The load-bearing decoys are gaps ALREADY covered by the existing tests (coverage-truth lens).

**Acceptance criteria.**
- Tests: full-recall case; missed hole fails 1; critical-severity trivia fails 2; inverted ordering fails 3; <2 pairs skip-passes 3; skeptic both labels; invalid JSON scores 0.
- No new dependencies (implement pairwise agreement locally; no scipy).
- `uv run pytest agents/evals` green; ruff clean.
depends_on: Evals: package scaffold with config and result models

boundedness.notes: in-session architect judgment (Fable) fills the boundedness seat

leaves.title: Ensemble: optional temperature on Prompt through ApiExecutor
leaves.content:
**What/why.** The eval runner needs pinned sampling (temperature 0) or pass-rates are noise, but `Prompt` (`agents/shared/ensemble/models.py`) has no temperature field and `ApiExecutor._call` (`agents/shared/ensemble/executor.py`) never passes one. Additive, default-off knob; Manual because agents/shared/ensemble sits under every agent.

**Files.** `agents/shared/ensemble/models.py`, `agents/shared/ensemble/executor.py`, plus matching tests under `agents/shared/ensemble/tests/`.

**Change.** `Prompt.temperature: float | None = None`. In `ApiExecutor._call`, include `temperature=prompt.temperature` in the anthropic `messages.create` and openai `chat.completions.create` kwargs ONLY when not None — when None the request kwargs are byte-identical to today (no `temperature` key at all).

**Acceptance criteria.**
- Default None -> request kwargs contain no temperature key (assert via mock/captured kwargs for both kinds); set -> passed through verbatim.
- Existing Prompt constructions all still validate (field has a default).
- Full `uv run pytest` green (whole repo — shared code); ruff clean.

boundedness.notes: worker-shaped but Manual by policy: agents/shared/ensemble is shared plumbing under every agent — never Auto (iron rule)

leaves.title: Evals: scorecard runner over the live router
leaves.content:
**What/why.** The scorecard runner: model id -> load gold sets -> render each case through its step adapter -> call the model through the SAME `Pool`/`ApiExecutor` stack production uses -> grade raw output -> aggregate to a `Scorecard`. CRITICAL DESIGN RULE: call `Pool.run` with NO `validate` callback. `panel.structured` retries schema-invalid output until the pool yields a valid payload — for production that is resilience, for an eval it is grade laundering. Transport retries stay; validity is graded from the first returned text of each repeat.

**Files.** `agents/evals/runner.py`, `agents/evals/report.py`, `agents/evals/tests/test_runner.py`.

**runner.py.**
- `GRADERS: dict[str, Callable[[GoldCase, str], GradeResult]]` mapping all 7 step keys to the graders (decomposition module serves decompose+boundedness; review serves both review keys; testgap both testgap keys).
- `run_scorecard(model: str, *, steps: list[str] | None = None, goldsets_root: Path | None = None, repeats: int | None = None, temperature: float | None = None, executor_factory=None) -> Scorecard`. Build one `ApiExecutor(kind="openai", model=model, base_url=settings.openai_base_url, api_key=settings.openai_api_key)` wrapped in a single-executor `Pool(role=f"evals:{model}")`; `executor_factory` (returning an `Executor`) is the test seam. Per case x repeat: `Prompt(system, user, max_tokens=settings.max_tokens, temperature=...)`, drive the pool via `asyncio.run` (mirror `panel.structured`'s sync wrapper), grade `result.output`; a transport-failed repeat -> `GradeResult(error=..., passed=False, score=0)`.
- Assemble `CaseScore` (holdout flag from the case) -> `StepScore` -> `Scorecard` (timestamp via `datetime.now(UTC).isoformat()`).

**report.py.** `render_scorecard(sc: Scorecard) -> str` (markdown: per-step table — cases, pass-rate, holdout pass-rate, mean score, error repeats; overall line) and `write_scorecard(sc, runs_dir) -> Path` persisting `scorecard.json` + `scorecard.md` under `runs_dir/<UTC-stamp>-<model>/`.

**Acceptance criteria.**
- With a scripted fake executor: valid outputs grade pass; an invalid-JSON repeat is graded fail (NOT retried — assert exactly `repeats` executor calls per case); a raising executor yields an error GradeResult; holdout split lands in StepScore; files written where promised.
- No live-network test; `uv run pytest agents/evals` green; ruff clean.
depends_on: Evals: gold case loader with schema version pinning
Evals: step adapters rendering production prompts
Evals: replan action grader
Evals: decomposition and boundedness grader
Evals: review findings grader
Evals: test gap severity grader
Ensemble: optional temperature on Prompt through ApiExecutor

boundedness.notes: in-session architect judgment (Fable) fills the boundedness seat

leaves.title: Evals: CLI with baseline capture and compare
leaves.content:
**What/why.** The front door: `meta evals ...` subcommands and the checked-in baseline mechanic that makes uplift MEASURED, not asserted.

**Files.** `agents/evals/main.py`, `agents/registry.py` (one new entry), `agents/evals/tests/test_main.py`.

**main.py** — argparse in the style of `agents/coding_pipeline/main.py`:
- `run` — `--model` (default settings.model), `--step` (repeatable, validates against the 7 step keys), `--goldsets`, `--repeats`; runs `run_scorecard`, writes via `report.write_scorecard`, prints the markdown, exit 0.
- `baseline` — same flags; additionally writes the scorecard JSON to `agents/evals/baselines/<model>.json` (mkdir ok; refuses to overwrite an existing baseline without `--force` — baselines are load-bearing history).
- `compare` — runs a fresh scorecard, loads `baselines/<model>.json` (missing -> exit 2 with a message naming `baseline`), prints a per-step delta table (pass-rate and holdout pass-rate deltas, regressions marked); exit 0 (advisory gate — humans read it).

**registry.py** — append `AgentCommand(name="evals", summary="Judgment eval harness: score models against frozen gold sets.", module="agents.evals.main", exposes_mcp=False)`.

**Acceptance criteria.**
- Tests (monkeypatched `run_scorecard`, tmp dirs): run writes + prints; baseline writes and refuses overwrite without --force; compare exits 2 without baseline and renders deltas with one; unknown --step errors naming the bad key; registry resolves `evals` and `load_main()` imports.
- `uv run pytest agents/evals agents/tests` green; ruff clean.
depends_on: Evals: scorecard runner over the live router

boundedness.notes: in-session architect judgment (Fable) fills the boundedness seat

leaves.title: Gold set: replan cases with frozen expected actions
leaves.content:
**What/why.** THE DISTILLATION (replan half). Freeze Fable-authored gold cases for the `replan` step in `agents/evals/goldsets/replan/<case-id>/`. Fable (in-session) mines real run artifacts and authors the inputs + expected-action rules; the coder tier must never write these.

**Sources (real first).** `pipeline-runs/build/wave-0002.json` (a real confirmed finding -> fixup case); `pipeline-runs/build/framing.json` + Forge tree for context; `pipeline-runs/smoke-tempconv/wave-000*.json` (failure/escalation material); the replan fix commit `3a6360cb` — its captured raw coder payload becomes (a) a grader unit-test fixture and (b) a negative few-shot exemplar later; the INPUTS that produced it become a case here.

**Coverage — at least 7 cases:** fixup-for-confirmed-finding; unconfirmed-finding-must-be- ignored (require_empty or must-not-fixup); escalated-leaf-hands-off (forbid_targets); clean-wave-empty-actions (require_empty); integration-red -> integration_fix (Manual + novel leaf); respec-under-cap with sharpened spec; halt-when-framing-invalidated. Mark >= 1/3 of cases `holdout: true` (never sourced for exemplars).

**Format.** Per case: `case.yaml` (schema_version 1, step replan, holdout, inputs map, expected block per the replan grader spec, notes explaining WHY the expected actions are right — the rationale is part of the distillation) + `framing.json`, `tree.json`, `report.json`, `attempts.json` as the adapter expects.

**Acceptance criteria.**
- `load_goldsets(root, "replan")` returns all cases; every case renders through the replan adapter without error.
- >= 7 cases, >= 1/3 holdout, >= 4 sourced from real run artifacts.
- Each case's notes field records the judgment rationale.
depends_on: Evals: gold case loader with schema version pinning
Evals: step adapters rendering production prompts

boundedness.notes: Manual by the oracle boundary: the expected-action rules ARE the distilled judgment — Fable authors them in-session; never delegate to the coder tier

leaves.title: Gold set: decomposition and boundedness cases
leaves.content:
**What/why.** THE DISTILLATION (decomposition half — the highest-leverage target: umbrella leaves are the #1 local-tier failure). Gold cases for `decompose` (framing+inventory in, rubric-checked tree out) and `boundedness` (labeled leaf specs).

**Sources.** Real framing+inventory pairs: `pipeline-runs/smoke-tempconv/`, `pipeline-runs/galleryapp-web-parity/`, `pipeline-runs/graphlib-consumer-proof/`, and `pipeline-runs/distill-evals/` itself (this epic — meta but legitimate). Real leaves from those `tree.json` files as worker-shaped boundedness positives; Fable authors umbrella counterexamples ("implement the backend", missing files, xl-in-disguise) as negatives.

**Coverage.** >= 3 decompose cases, each with a Fable-authored rubric in `expected.rubric` using the grader DSL — e.g. require_manual for gold-authoring-like judgment leaves, require_leaf for the load-bearing slice, forbid_leaf for known umbrella phrasings, min/max_leaves sanity bounds, require_dep for the must-be-sequenced edge. >= 8 boundedness cases (mixed labels, >= 3 umbrella negatives, including one plausible-but-unshaped trap). >= 1/3 of each holdout.

**Acceptance criteria.**
- All cases load and render through their adapters.
- Every rubric item's `id` is unique within its case and every rubric kind is one the grader implements (loader/grader raise otherwise).
- Rationale in each case's notes.
depends_on: Evals: gold case loader with schema version pinning
Evals: step adapters rendering production prompts

boundedness.notes: Manual by the oracle boundary: rubric checklists and worker-shaped labels are the distilled judgment — Fable-authored

leaves.title: Gold set: review findings cases over seeded bug diffs
leaves.content:
**What/why.** THE DISTILLATION (review half). Known-buggy diffs with frozen reference finding sets (`review-findings`) and labeled confirm-vote candidates (`review-confirm`).

**Sources.** Real wave diffs reconstructed from run history (jj revisions around the build and smoke epics) as clean or known-finding cases; Fable-seeded diffs for coverage: plant one bug per class across cases — semantic inversion (the known auto-tier failure signature), off-by-one, swallowed exception, resource leak on an error path, a test that asserts the bug. Keep each diff < ~300 lines so grading stays sharp.

**Coverage.** 4-6 `review-findings` cases: each with `expected.must_find` match rules (file_pattern + keywords) for every planted bug, thresholds tuned per case; include one CLEAN diff where the right answer is an empty findings list (precision discipline — "don't manufacture issues"). 6+ `review-confirm` cases over those diffs: real findings (expected.real=true) and decoys — style nit, pre-existing issue, matter of taste (expected.real=false). >= 1/3 holdout across both.

**Acceptance criteria.**
- All cases load and render; every planted bug documented in notes (class, file, line area, why the match rule identifies it).
- The clean-diff case is present and its expected block accepts only an empty list.
depends_on: Evals: gold case loader with schema version pinning
Evals: step adapters rendering production prompts

boundedness.notes: Manual by the oracle boundary: planted-bug inventories and real/decoy labels are the distilled judgment — Fable-authored

leaves.title: Gold set: test gap cases with severity rankings
leaves.content:
**What/why.** THE DISTILLATION (test-gap half). Source+tests fixture pairs with frozen gap inventories and severity ORDERINGS (`testgap-find`), plus labeled skeptic verdicts (`testgap-skeptic`). The judgment frozen here: rank the real hole above the trivia.

**Sources.** Snapshot small real-ish modules INTO the fixture dirs (frozen copies, never live repo paths — live code rots the gold): e.g. a trimmed slugify/automerge-style module, a retry/timeout wrapper, a parser with error paths. Author the existing-tests file to deliberately cover some behaviors and miss others.

**Coverage.** 3-4 `testgap-find` cases; per case: `must_find` (the real holes — error paths, boundary, concurrency-ish ordering assumption), `nice_to_find` (legitimate but minor), a reference severity ordering over must_find, an `angle` input selecting the FINDER_ANGLES entry, thresholds. At least one case whose existing tests are thorough — correct answer is (nearly) no gaps, catching the cry-wolf failure. 6+ `testgap-skeptic` cases: claimed gaps labeled real=true, and the load-bearing decoys — gaps ALREADY covered by the existing tests (coverage-truth lens), labeled real=false. >= 1/3 holdout.

**Acceptance criteria.**
- All cases load and render through both testgap adapters.
- Severity orderings are total over must_find entries; rationale in notes.
depends_on: Evals: gold case loader with schema version pinning
Evals: step adapters rendering production prompts

boundedness.notes: Manual by the oracle boundary: the gap inventory and severity ordering are the distilled judgment — Fable-authored

leaves.title: Baseline: capture coder scorecard before prompt changes
leaves.content:
**What/why.** Baseline discipline (framing constraint 3): capture the local coder tier's scorecard BEFORE any prompt change lands, so distillation uplift is measured against a frozen before. This leaf BLOCKS both Distill leaves.

**Steps.**
1. `meta evals baseline --model coder` against the full gold sets (all 7 step keys), default repeats (3) and temperature 0 over the live router (llm-router.internal:4010).
2. Eyeball `error_repeats` per step — transport flakes above ~10% mean re-run before freezing; a noisy baseline is not a baseline.
3. Commit `agents/evals/baselines/coder.json`. In the scorecard `notes`, record the date and the concrete router model the `coder` alias resolved to (from the router config on the router host) — alias drift must be visible later.
4. Optional but cheap: also capture `baselines/<one-other-tier>.json` for contrast if the router exposes one worth tracking.

**Acceptance criteria.**
- `agents/evals/baselines/coder.json` committed; all 7 steps present with >= 1 case each; error repeats within tolerance; notes name the resolved router model and date.
depends_on: Evals: CLI with baseline capture and compare
Gold set: replan cases with frozen expected actions
Gold set: decomposition and boundedness cases
Gold set: review findings cases over seeded bug diffs
Gold set: test gap cases with severity rankings

boundedness.notes: Manual: needs the live router and a human eye on flaky repeats; it also commits the load-bearing baseline artifact

leaves.title: Distill: architect prompts with few-shot exemplars
leaves.content:
**What/why.** Land the distilled judgment into the architect prompts: tighten `DECOMPOSE_SYSTEM`, `BOUNDEDNESS_SYSTEM`, `REPLAN_SYSTEM` (and `FRAMING_SYSTEM` only if a cheap win is obvious) in `agents/coding_pipeline/architect.py`. PROMPT TEXT ONLY — no logic/behavior changes (framing constraint 4).

**Method.**
- Few-shot exemplars sourced ONLY from non-holdout gold cases (framing constraint 5) plus the 3a6360cb raw payload as the canonical negative (what invalid replan output looks like and why it was discarded).
- Bake the rubric language in: what umbrella leaves look like and how to split them; worker-shaped criteria with a positive and negative exemplar each; replan action-choice guidance (when respec vs escalate vs halt — mirror the gold rationale notes).
- Watch the token budget: exemplars inflate every A2/A4 call; keep additions <= ~40% of current prompt length and note the before/after prompt sizes in the commit message.

**Files.** `agents/coding_pipeline/architect.py`; update any prompt-content assertions in `agents/coding_pipeline/tests/test_architect.py`.

**Acceptance criteria.**
- `meta evals compare --model coder`: HOLDOUT pass-rate on replan + decompose + boundedness at or above baseline with at least one step materially up; NO step regresses on holdout.
- Full `uv run pytest` green; ruff clean on changed files.
- Scorecard delta pasted into the task notes when closing (the measured uplift record).
depends_on: Baseline: capture coder scorecard before prompt changes

boundedness.notes: Manual by the oracle boundary: the exemplars ARE the captured judgment; prompt-text-only change measured by scorecard delta

leaves.title: Distill: review and testgap prompts with few-shot exemplars
leaves.content:
**What/why.** Land the distilled judgment into the review and test-gap prompts: `FINDINGS_SYSTEM`, `CONFIRM_SYSTEM`, `CONSOLIDATE_SYSTEM` in `agents/coding_pipeline/verify.py`, and the finder/skeptic/dedup prompts in `agents/testing_ensemble/prompts.py`. PROMPT TEXT ONLY.

**Method.**
- Exemplars from non-holdout gold only: a real-bug finding done right (specific file + concrete defect), a decoy correctly rejected by the skeptic (pre-existing issue / style nit / already-covered gap), and a severity-ordering exemplar (why the error-path hole outranks the boundary nit).
- Sharpen the confirm skeptic's default-to-not-real stance with the decoy taxonomy from the gold notes; sharpen the coverage-truth lens with an already-covered exemplar.
- `pr_review_ensemble/prompts.py` (markdown-output prompts) is OUT of scope — ungraded surface, separate pass later.

**Files.** `agents/coding_pipeline/verify.py`, `agents/testing_ensemble/prompts.py`; update prompt-content assertions in their test dirs if any.

**Acceptance criteria.**
- `meta evals compare --model coder`: HOLDOUT pass-rate on review-findings + review-confirm + testgap-find + testgap-skeptic at or above baseline with at least one step materially up; NO step regresses on holdout.
- Full `uv run pytest` green; ruff clean on changed files.
- Scorecard delta pasted into the task notes when closing.
depends_on: Baseline: capture coder scorecard before prompt changes

boundedness.notes: Manual by the oracle boundary: exemplar authoring is the captured judgment; prompt-text-only change measured by scorecard delta

leaves.title: Docs: eval harness workflow and baseline policy
leaves.content:
**What/why.** The harness only stays alive if the workflow is written down: when to re-score, how baselines move, and the holdout rule that keeps uplift honest.

**Files.** `agents/evals/README.md` (new), `DEVELOPMENT-WORKFLOWS.md` (new section), optionally `agents/coding_pipeline/README.md` (one cross-link line).

**README.md.** Package map (fixtures/steps/graders/runner/report/main); the goldset dir layout + case.yaml fields with one full worked example; the 7 step keys and which production prompt each measures; how to add a gold case; the schema_version rule.

**DEVELOPMENT-WORKFLOWS.md section — "Judgment evals (meta evals)".** The loop: any change to a graded prompt or a router model swap -> `meta evals compare --model coder` -> paste the delta into the PR/task. Baseline update policy: baselines move only via `meta evals baseline --force` after a human signs off on a verified uplift — never casually, they are the regression reference. Holdout policy: exemplars may only cite non-holdout cases; holdout is what compare judges. Cross-reference the distill-evals epic and `architect-brief.md` for the why.

**Acceptance criteria.**
- Both files exist with the named sections; commands are copy-paste runnable; ruff/format untouched elsewhere. No code changes.
depends_on: Evals: CLI with baseline capture and compare

boundedness.notes: docs leaf: acceptance = named files and sections exist; suite-neutral
